codeReview.summary: The LanceDB vector store implementation exists in crates/swell-memory/src/lancedb_vector.rs (29,163 bytes) and compiles successfully with 14 passing tests. However, the feature 'mem-lancedb-integration' is NOT registered in the mission's features.json, and no handoff or worker transcript exists for this feature. Additionally, the recall@10 >= 0.9 target specified in the feature description is not verified by any test.

issues.description: Feature 'mem-lancedb-integration' is not registered in .factory/missions/67c2b690-f32a-4538-8ff8-3eb2790dc44e/features.json. The LanceDB implementation exists but was not tracked as a feature in the mission system.

No handoff or worker transcript exists for this feature. The mission's handoffs/ directory and worker-transcripts.jsonl are missing, making it impossible to verify the worker's skill usage, procedure adherence, or code review decisions.

The recall@10 >= 0.9 target from the feature description is not verified by tests. test_recall_with_structured_vectors only asserts recall >= 0.5, not the stated 0.9 threshold. The test_comment on line 519 mentions 'We expect lower recall than brute-force baseline because of quantization' but this conflicts with the feature requirement of >= 0.9.

LanceDbVectorConfig::default() sets distance_type to Cosine, but LanceDbDistanceType::default() is L2. This inconsistency in defaults could cause confusion. The config default should probably be L2 to match the enum default, or the enum default should be Cosine.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature 'mem-lancedb-integration' does not exist in the mission's features.json, yet the implementation exists in lancedb_vector.rs. This indicates either: (1) the feature was implemented outside the mission workflow, or (2) it was registered in a different mission or milestone.
No handoffs/ or worker-transcripts.jsonl exist in the mission directory for this feature. The standard scrutiny workflow requires these artifacts to verify skill usage and procedure adherence.

The recall@10 >= 0.9 requirement from the feature description is not tested. The test_recall_with_structured_vectors test only checks for recall >= 0.5. This gap means we cannot confirm the ANN search meets its stated performance target.

summary: The LanceDB vector store implementation in lancedb_vector.rs compiles and passes 14 tests. However, this review cannot fully validate the feature because: (1) it is not registered in features.json, (2) no handoff/transcript exists, and (3) the recall@10 >= 0.9 performance target is not verified. The code quality appears sound but the feature bypassed the standard mission workflow.
